2604.04475.title: Discrete Prototypical Memories for Federated Time Series Foundation Models
2604.04475.paper_summary: Leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) as federated learning (FL)-based time series foundation models offers a promising way to transfer the generalization capabilities of LLMs to time series data while preserving access to private data. However, the semantic misalignment between time-series data and the text-centric latent space of existing LLMs often leads to degraded performance. Meanwhile, the parameter-sharing mechanism in existing FL methods model heterogeneous cross-domain time-series data into a unified continuous latent space, which contradicts the fact that time-series semantics frequently manifest as discrete and recurring regimes. To address these limitations, we propose \textsc{FeDPM}, a federated framework for time-series foundation models based on discrete prototypical memories. Specifically, we learn local prototypical memory priors for intra-domain time-series data. We then align cross-domain memories to promote a unified discrete latent space and introduce a domain-specific memory update mechanism to balance shared and personalized prototypical knowledge. Extensive experiments demonstrate the efficiency and effectiveness of \textsc{FeDPM}. The code is publicly available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FedUnit-64D1.
2604.04475.paper_summary_zh: 利用大型語言模型（LLMs）作為基於聯邦學習（FL）的時間序列基礎模型，提供了一種有前景的方法，可以將LLMs的泛化能力轉移到時間序列數據，同時保護私有數據的訪問。然而，時間序列數據與現有LLMs的以文本為中心的潛在空間之間的語義不對齊，往往導致性能下降。與此同時，現有FL方法中的參數共享機制將異質的跨域時間序列數據建模為統一的連續潛在空間，這與時間序列語義經常以離散和重複的模式表現出來的事實相矛盾。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了\textsc{FeDPM}，一個基於離散原型記憶的時間序列基礎模型的聯邦框架。具體而言，我們為域內時間序列數據學習局部原型記憶先驗。然後，我們對齊跨域記憶，以促進統一的離散潛在空間，並引入一種特定於域的記憶更新機制，以平衡共享和個性化的原型知識。大量實驗證明了\textsc{FeDPM}的效率和有效性。代碼可在 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FedUnit-64D1 上公開獲取。
2604.04475.author: Liwei Deng et.al.
2604.04475.authors: Liwei Deng, Qingxiang Liu, Xinhe Niu, Shengchao Chen, Sheng Sun, Yuankai Wu, Guodong Long, Yuxuan Liang
